questions.q: Which deity takes the form of Tripurantaka in the Puranas?
questions.explanation: Tripurantaka is the form Shiva assumed to destroy the three cities of the asuras.

questions.q: The name Tripurantaka refers to Shiva as the destroyer of what?
questions.explanation: Tripurantaka means 'destroyer of Tripura,' the three flying cities of the asuras.

questions.q: How many cities made up Tripura, which Shiva as Tripurantaka destroyed?
questions.explanation: Tripura consisted of three cities, which is the meaning of the prefix 'tri' in the name.

questions.q: According to the Puranas, the three cities of Tripura were built of gold, silver, and which third metal?
questions.explanation: The three cities of Tripura were made of gold, silver, and iron.

questions.q: Who built the three flying cities of Tripura in the Tripurantaka legend?
questions.explanation: The asura architect Maya constructed the three cities of Tripura.

questions.q: In the Tripurantaka episode, the inhabitants of the three cities of Tripura were chiefly which kind of beings?
questions.explanation: The three cities of Tripura were the strongholds of the asuras.

questions.q: With how many arrows did Shiva as Tripurantaka destroy the three cities of Tripura?
questions.explanation: Shiva loosed a single arrow to burn all three cities at once.

questions.q: What weapon does Shiva wield as Tripurantaka in the destruction of the three cities?
questions.explanation: As Tripurantaka, Shiva wields a great bow and a single arrow.

questions.q: Which mountain served as Shiva's bow when he destroyed Tripura as Tripurantaka?
questions.explanation: The Puranas describe Mount Meru as the bow Shiva used against the three cities.

questions.q: The Tripurantaka episode of Shiva burning the three cities is recounted chiefly in which body of texts?
questions.explanation: The destruction of Tripura is narrated in the Puranas, with roots in earlier Vedic and epic tradition.

questions.q: In the Tripurantaka legend, what served as Shiva's chariot when he set out to destroy the three cities?
questions.explanation: The Puranas describe Shiva riding a chariot fashioned from the earth itself to destroy Tripura.

questions.q: According to the Puranic account, Shiva could destroy the three cities of Tripura only at what precise moment?
options: When the cities aligned into one
questions.explanation: Tripura could be destroyed only when its three cities aligned for an instant, and Shiva struck then.

questions.q: In the Tripura legend, which deity granted the asuras the boon and had the three cities built after their long penance?
questions.explanation: After the austerities of Taraka's sons, Brahma granted them the boon and the three cities of Tripura were raised by the architect Maya.

questions.q: In Hindu iconography, the form of Shiva drawing his bow to destroy the three cities is known by which name?
questions.explanation: The bow-wielding warrior aspect of Shiva that destroys Tripura is iconographically named Tripurantaka.

questions.q: In the Tripurantaka legend, the architect Maya who built the three cities belonged to which class of beings?
questions.explanation: Maya was the celebrated architect of the asuras, builder of the three cities of Tripura.

questions.q: Tripurantaka is a warrior aspect of which member of the Trimurti?
questions.explanation: Tripurantaka is an aspect of Shiva, the destroyer within the Trimurti.

questions.q: In the Tripurantaka episode, the gods approached Shiva because they could not, by themselves, overcome whom?
options: The asuras of the three cities
questions.explanation: The devas sought Shiva's aid because they could not defeat the asuras dwelling in the three cities of Tripura.

questions.q: By what means did Shiva as Tripurantaka ultimately consume the three cities of Tripura?
questions.explanation: Shiva's single arrow burned the three cities of Tripura to ashes in an instant.

questions.q: Within the Trimurti scheme, the role of destruction expressed by Shiva as Tripurantaka is called by which Sanskrit term?
questions.explanation: Samhara is the cosmic function of destruction associated with Shiva, exemplified in the Tripura legend.

questions.q: In the Tripurantaka narrative, the three cities were said to roam through which domain?
questions.explanation: The three cities of Tripura were flying cities that moved through the sky.

questions.q: Which mount is traditionally associated with Shiva, the deity who appears as Tripurantaka?
questions.explanation: Shiva's vahana is the bull Nandi, and Shiva is the deity who takes the Tripurantaka form.

questions.q: Who is the consort of Shiva, the deity revered as Tripurantaka?
questions.explanation: Parvati is the consort of Shiva, who takes the Tripurantaka form to destroy the three cities.

questions.q: In Puranic accounts of the Tripura war, which deity is often said to serve as the charioteer of Shiva's earth-chariot?
questions.explanation: Several Puranic versions describe Brahma acting as the charioteer of Shiva's chariot in the destruction of Tripura.

questions.q: In the Tripurantaka legend, which deity is described as forming the arrow with which Shiva burned the three cities?
questions.explanation: The Puranas relate that Vishnu became the shaft of Shiva's arrow, with Agni at its tip, to destroy Tripura.

questions.q: According to the Puranas, the devotion of the asuras of the three cities to which deity long protected them before their fall?
questions.explanation: The asuras of Tripura were portrayed as devotees of Shiva, whose grace long shielded their cities.

questions.q: In the Tripura account, which luminary deities are described as serving as the wheels of Shiva's cosmic chariot?
questions.explanation: The Puranas describe the sun and moon as the two wheels of Shiva's chariot in the Tripura legend.

questions.q: In the Tripura narrative, the architect Maya was the builder of the three cities for the sons of which asura?
questions.explanation: The three cities were built for the three sons of the asura Taraka, Tarakaksha, Vidyunmali, and Kamalaksha.

questions.q: In Puranic accounts, what served as the bowstring of Mount Meru when Shiva strung it to destroy Tripura?
options: A strand of the goddess Ganga
questions.explanation: The serpent Vasuki is described as the bowstring of Shiva's mountain-bow in the Tripura legend.

questions.q: The destruction of Tripura by Shiva as Tripurantaka has its earliest roots in which body of sacred literature?
questions.explanation: The Tripura motif appears in the Vedic and Brahmana literature before being elaborated in the Puranas.

questions.q: The form of Shiva as Tripurantaka is theologically grouped among which set of his fierce, destructive aspects?
questions.explanation: Tripurantaka is counted among the Samhara-murtis, the destructive or annihilating forms of Shiva.
